title: NIS2 Risk Analysis Evidence for Important Entities

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIS2 Art.21(2) — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.22: NIS2 Risk Analysis Evidence for Important Entities.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.22' running on nis2_entity_classification.csv (entity_tier=important), qualys:hostdetection, authentication CIM, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for Qualys (Splunkbase 2964)
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_entity_classification.csv WHERE entity_tier="important"
| rename asset_hostname as host
| join host [
    search index=qualys sourcetype="qualys:hostdetection" earliest=-30d
    | eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(first_found_datetime,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"))/86400,0)
    | where severity IN ("Critical","High") AND age_days > 30
    | stats dc(qid) as aged_findings by host
]
| where aged_findings > 0
| table host, business_service, aged_findings
| sort - aged_findings
description: Important entities must implement appropriate and proportionate measures. This use case applies lighter-weight but documented risk indicators — patch latency, authentication anomalies, and backup health — tailored to important-entity thresholds so compliance teams can demonstrate proportionality without over-burdening smaller operators.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes qualys and sourcetypes qualys:hostdetection. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Set proportionate SLAs for important entities (e.g., 30-day critical patch target); (2) ingest Qualys or Tenable with host CMDB mapping; (3) monthly management review of aged findings; (4) pair with UC-22.2.21 for entities that may be reclassified; (5) document risk acceptance where exceptions exist. Indexes required: `index=qualys`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=qualys:hostdetection`; Lookups: `nis2_entity_classification`, `nis2_entity_classification.csv`.
visualization: Bar chart (aged findings by host), Table (top offenders), Single value (hosts past SLA).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.dest, Vulnerabilities.severity | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Operational noise during large maintenance windows, controlled exercises, or scope changes to critical entity lists.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for Qualys (Splunkbase 2964).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `nis2_entity_classification.csv` (entity_tier=important), `qualys:hostdetection`, authentication CIM.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Set proportionate SLAs for important entities (e.g., 30-day critical patch target); (2) ingest Qualys or Tenable with host CMDB mapping; (3) monthly management review of aged findings; (4) pair with UC-22.2.21 for entities that may be reclassified; (5) document risk acceptance where exceptions exist.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_entity_classification.csv WHERE entity_tier="important"
| rename asset_hostname as host
| join host [
    search index=qualys sourcetype="qualys:hostdetection" earliest=-30d
    | eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(first_found_datetime,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"))/86400,0)
    | where severity IN ("Critical","High") AND age_days > 30
    | stats dc(qid) as aged_findings by host
]
| where aged_findings > 0
| table host, business_service, aged_findings
| sort - aged_findings
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 Risk Analysis Evidence for Important Entities (Art. 21(2))** — Important entities must implement appropriate and proportionate measures. This use case applies lighter-weight but documented risk indicators — patch latency, authentication anomalies, and backup health — tailored to important-entity thresholds so compliance teams can demonstrate proportionality without over-burdening smaller operators.

Documented **Data sources**: `nis2_entity_classification.csv` (entity_tier=important), `qualys:hostdetection`, authentication CIM. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for Qualys (Splunkbase 2964). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Renames fields with `rename` for clarity or joins.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where aged_findings > 0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

See also the **CIM SPL** field in this use case; enable the matching data model acceleration if you run the tstats variant in production.

Output fields: age_days, aged_findings, business_service, host.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the vulnerability scanner console (Tenable, Qualys, or your standard tool) for the same asset and scan window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (aged findings by host), Table (top offenders), Single value (hosts past SLA).
grandmaExplanation: We important entities must implement appropriate and proportionate measures.
